GI Stasis.note: Cisapride/metoclopramide ± meloxicam, SC/IV fluids, critical-care feeding, treat the cause

Dental Disease / Malocclusion.correct: Crown reduction (กรอฟัน) under GA + analgesia
Dental Disease / Malocclusion.note: Burr down spurs/elongated crowns, pain control, shift to hay-first diet

Encephalitozoonosis (E. cuniculi).note: Fenbendazole 20 mg/kg PO q24h × 28 d; ± anti-inflammatory, supportive care (Asia Pet/BSAVA)

Pasteurellosis (Snuffles).note: Enrofloxacin or TMS, long course; correct ammonia/airflow (Asia Pet)

Uterine Adenocarcinoma.correct: Ovariohysterectomy (ผ่าตัดทำหมัน)
Uterine Adenocarcinoma.note: OVH curative if pre-metastatic; thoracic staging first

Sore Hocks (Pododermatitis).note: Clean/bandage, solid soft bedding, weight control, pain control; abx if infected

Heat Stroke.note: Cool gradually (NOT ice), IV fluids, oxygen, monitor for DIC/organ failure

RHD (Rabbit Hemorrhagic Disease).note: No cure; intensive supportive care, isolate; prevention = RHDV2 vaccine + biosecurity

Scurvy (Vitamin C Deficiency).note: Vitamin C 30–50 mg/kg/day PO; correct diet (fresh veg/pellets with stable C)

Ovarian Cysts.note: OVH definitive; ± hCG/GnRH or deslorelin as temporising

Dental Disease — Chinchilla (Slobbers).note: Burr cheek-tooth spurs, pain control, increase hay/chewing

Wet Tail (Proliferative Ileitis).note: Aggressive fluids, enrofloxacin/TMS, warmth; high mortality

Insulinoma.note: Manage hypoglycemia: small frequent meals, prednisolone, ± diazoxide; nodulectomy

Adrenal Disease.note: Deslorelin (Suprelorin) implant suppresses signs; surgery for unilateral (Asia Pet)

Lymphoma — Ferret.note: Multi-agent chemo protocols; prednisolone palliative; staging first

Wobbly Hedgehog Syndrome (WHS).note: Progressive neurodegenerative; supportive nursing, padded housing; grave prognosis

Hibernation Attempt.note: Warm slowly to 24–30°C, warmed fluids, support glucose; correct husbandry temp

Hindlimb Paralysis (HLP).note: Balance diet (reduce insectivore/fruit excess), calcium/UVB; guarded if advanced

Psittacosis (Chlamydia).note: Doxycycline prolonged (≈45 d); ZOONOTIC — owner precautions (Asia Pet)

PBFD (Psittacine Beak & Feather Disease).note: No specific antiviral; supportive, biosecurity, test flock; guarded

Avian Aspergillosis.note: Long azole therapy ± amphotericin B nebulisation; improve ventilation (Asia Pet)

Egg Binding (Dystocia) — Avian.correct: Calcium + warmth/humidity; oxytocin/PGE; surgery if needed
Egg Binding (Dystocia) — Avian.note: Stabilise + calcium, warmth/fluids; if non-obstructive oxytocin; ovocentesis/surgery if obstructive

Heavy Metal Toxicity.note: CaEDTA chelation; remove source/GI metal; supportive (lead/zinc) (Asia Pet)

Teflon Toxicity (PTFE Fume Fever).note: Fresh air/oxygen, anti-inflammatory; frequently fatal — prevention key

Hypocalcemia — African Grey.note: Calcium gluconate IM/slow IV for seizures; long-term diet+UVB correction (Asia Pet)

Metabolic Bone Disease (MBD).note: Correct husbandry: UVB, dietary Ca:P; calcium support; treat fractures gently

Mouth Rot (Infectious Stomatitis).note: Debride caseous plaques, culture-guided abx, fix temperature/husbandry

Reptile Respiratory Infection (RI).note: Systemic abx (ceftazidime/enrofloxacin), correct basking temp/humidity, fluids

Reptile Egg Binding (Dystocia).note: Provide nest/temp/Ca; oxytocin for non-obstructive; ovocentesis/coeliotomy if obstructive

Hypovitaminosis A — Chelonian.note: Cautious vitamin A (avoid hypervitaminosis), correct diet (dark leafy greens)

Shell Rot (Ulcerative Shell Disease).note: Clean/debride, topical antiseptic, systemic abx if deep, correct water quality

Chytridiomycosis.note: Itraconazole bath protocol; elevated temperature; biosecurity (Asia Pet)

Canine Parvovirus.note: IV fluids, antiemetics, broad-spectrum abx for sepsis, nutrition; no antiviral

Canine Distemper.note: Supportive: fluids, abx for secondary infection, anticonvulsants; prevention = vaccine (Asia Pet)

Heartworm Disease.note: Staged adulticide protocol + doxycycline + macrocyclic lactone; strict rest (Asia Pet)

Feline Lower Urinary Tract Disease (FLUT.correct: Unblock if obstructed + fluids + diet/stress mgmt
Feline Lower Urinary Tract Disease (FLUT.note: Relieve obstruction (EMERGENCY in toms), fluids, analgesia, wet diet, reduce stress

Chronic Kidney Disease (Cat CKD).note: Renal diet, SC/IV fluids, phosphate binders, manage BP/anemia; not curable (Asia Pet)

Feline Hyperthyroidism.note: Methimazole long-term; radioiodine curative; monitor renal unmasking (Asia Pet)

Diabetes Mellitus.note: Species-appropriate insulin + diet; monitor glucose curves; treat ketoacidosis if present

Otitis Externa.correct: Clean + topical (based on cytology) + treat cause
Otitis Externa.note: Ear cleaning + topical antimicrobial/antiparasitic per cytology; address underlying (Asia Pet)

Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD).note: Cage rest + analgesia for mild; surgical decompression if non-ambulatory/no deep pain

Mammary Tumors.note: Wide excision/mastectomy; histopath + thoracic staging; spay reduces risk

Cushing's Disease (HAC).note: Trilostane for PDH; surgery for adrenal-dependent; monitor closely

FeLV (Feline Leukemia Virus).note: No cure; manage secondary infections/anemia/neoplasia; isolate; prevention = vaccine

Pyometra.note: OVH is treatment of choice after IV stabilisation; medical only if breeding+open

Cryptosporidiosis.note: No consistently effective cure; supportive, strict hygiene; guarded (reptile/bird)

Atherosclerosis.note: Reduce dietary fat, weight control, manage cardiac signs; largely irreversible (bird)

Macrorhabdus (Megabacteria).note: Oral amphotericin B course; supportive feeding; lower environmental pH

Chocolate Toxicity.note: Emesis/charcoal if early, IV fluids, control tachyarrhythmia/seizures (Asia Pet)

Xylitol Toxicity.note: IV dextrose for hypoglycemia, monitor liver values, hepatoprotectants (Asia Pet)

Lily Toxicity.note: Early decontamination + 48–72 h IV fluids (cat AKI emergency) (Asia Pet)

Ferret GI Foreign Body.note: Exploratory surgery or endoscopy to remove FB; fluids/analgesia

Liver Lobe Torsion.note: Stabilise then surgical removal of torsed lobe; analgesia/fluids (rabbit)

Flystrike (Myiasis).note: Manually remove larvae, clip/clean wounds, analgesia, treat shock; prevent recurrence

Splay Leg.note: Limb hobbling, padded footing, physiotherapy if young; correct flooring

Coccidiosis (Rabbit).note: Ponazuril or toltrazuril, fluids, hygiene; treat hepatic vs intestinal form (Asia Pet)

Urolithiasis / Bladder Sludge — Rabbit.note: Cystotomy for true stones, flush sludge, increase water, reduce dietary Ca, analgesia

Pseudopregnancy (Rabbit).note: Self-resolves in ~2–3 weeks; reduce triggers; OVH if recurrent

Pregnancy Toxemia (Guinea Pig).note: IV glucose/fluids, propylene glycol, assisted feeding; guarded; prevent via late-gestation nutrition

Trixacarus Mange (Guinea Pig).note: Ivermectin or selamectin repeated; analgesia (intensely pruritic) (Asia Pet)

Epizootic Catarrhal Enteritis (ECE).note: Fluids, nutritional support, ± abx for secondary; usually self-limiting in adults (ferret)

Estrogen Toxicity (Intact Jill).correct: Spay + hormone control; transfusion if severe
Estrogen Toxicity (Intact Jill).note: Remove estrogen source (OVH/hCG/deslorelin); transfusion for severe marrow suppression (Asia Pet)

Hedgehog Mites (Caparinia).note: Ivermectin or selamectin repeated; treat environment (Asia Pet)

Hedgehog Cardiomyopathy (DCM).note: Pimobendan + furosemide for CHF signs; guarded prognosis (Asia Pet)

Sour Crop / Crop Stasis.note: Empty/lavage crop, treat yeast (nystatin/fluconazole), fluids/heat (Asia Pet)

Feather Destructive Behavior (FDB).correct: Enrichment/behavior mgmt after ruling out disease
Feather Destructive Behavior (FDB).note: Address husbandry/behavior/enrichment once medical causes excluded

Hepatic Lipidosis (Bird).note: Critical-care feeding, fluids, ± lactulose/carnitine; convert off all-seed diet

Avian Gout.correct: Allopurinol + fluids + lower protein; correct husbandry
Avian Gout.note: Allopurinol, fluids/hydration, reduce dietary protein, fix renal cause (Asia Pet)

Goiter (Budgerigar).note: Iodine (Lugol's) supplementation, convert off all-seed diet

Bumblefoot (Avian Pododermatitis).correct: Wound care + correct perches + analgesia ± abx
Bumblefoot (Avian Pododermatitis).note: Debride/bandage, soft varied perching, weight control, abx if deep (Asia Pet)

Trichomoniasis (Canker).note: Metronidazole or carnidazole course; supportive (Asia Pet)

Cnemidocoptes (Scaly Face/Leg Mite).note: Ivermectin repeated; treat cagemates; usually good response (Asia Pet)

Reptile Gout.note: Allopurinol, aggressive hydration, correct hydration/protein/temperature (Asia Pet)

Snake Mites (Ophionyssus).note: Treat snake (ivermectin/selamectin) + decontaminate enclosure (NOT ivermectin in chelonians) (Asia Pet)

Inclusion Body Disease (IBD).note: No cure (boid arenavirus); supportive, strict quarantine; euthanasia often advised

Atadenovirus (Bearded Dragon).note: Supportive husbandry/nutrition, treat secondary infections, isolate

Yellow Fungus Disease (CANV).note: Systemic antifungal long course + surgical debridement; guarded (Asia Pet)

Gastric Dilatation-Volvulus (GDV).note: Shock fluids, gastric decompression, immediate surgery + gastropexy (Asia Pet)

Canine Atopic Dermatitis.note: Anti-itch (oclacitinib/lokivetmab), bathing, omega-3, allergen control; manage 2° infection

Anal Sac Disease.note: Express sacs; infected/impacted → flush + abx; abscess → lance/drain

Periodontal Disease.correct: Dental scaling/extractions under GA + home care
Periodontal Disease.note: Professional dental, extract diseased teeth, home dental care

Cranial Cruciate Ligament Rupture (CCL).note: Surgery (TPLO/extracapsular) for most; weight control + rehab

Tick-Borne Disease.note: Doxycycline (ehrlichia/anaplasma); supportive ± transfusion; tick prevention (Asia Pet)

Canine Pancreatitis.note: IV fluids, analgesia, maropitant, early enteral nutrition, low-fat diet

Brachycephalic Obstructive Airway Syndro.note: Surgical correction (nares/soft palate), weight loss, avoid heat/stress

Tracheal Collapse.note: Cough suppressants, weight loss, harness; sedation for crises; stent/surgery refractory

Kennel Cough (CIRD).note: Usually self-limiting; doxycycline if febrile/productive; isolate; vaccinate

Feline Hepatic Lipidosis.note: Early feeding tube nutrition is key, fluids, anti-emetics, treat primary disease

Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy (HCM).correct: Manage CHF (furosemide) ± clopidogrel for ATE risk
Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy (HCM).note: Furosemide for CHF, manage thromboembolism risk (clopidogrel); not curable

Feline Asthma.note: Glucocorticoids (inhaled fluticasone or systemic) + bronchodilator; reduce allergens

Feline IBD / Triaditis.note: Hydrolysed/novel diet, prednisolone ± B12; biopsy to confirm vs lymphoma

Dysbiosis / Fermentation Failure.correct: High-fiber diet + fluids; avoid wrong antibiotics
Dysbiosis / Fermentation Failure.note: Restore hay/fiber, fluids, prokinetics; cholestyramine if enterotoxemia risk; correct diet (Asia Pet)

Trichobezoar (Hairballs).note: Aggressive fluids + critical-care feeding to rehydrate/move mass; surgery if obstructive

Proventricular Dilatation Disease (PDD).note: COX-2 inhibitor (celecoxib/meloxicam), soft diet, supportive; guarded (bornavirus)

GI Impaction (Reptile).correct: Warm soaks + fluids + correct husbandry (surgery if severe)
GI Impaction (Reptile).note: Hydration/warm soaks, fluids, fix substrate/temp; enterotomy if true obstruction

Ear Mites — Rabbit (Psoroptes).note: Ivermectin or selamectin repeated; analgesia; soften — never pull crusts (Asia Pet)

Ear Base Abscess.note: Total ear canal/bulla surgery or marsupialisation, long abx by culture, analgesia

Dacryocystitis (Rabbit).correct: Nasolacrimal flush + treat tooth root cause ± abx
Dacryocystitis (Rabbit).note: Flush nasolacrimal duct, topical/systemic abx, address incisor root impingement

Egg Yolk Peritonitis (Bird).note: Fluids/abx, drain coelom, ± surgery; deslorelin/hormonal control to stop laying (Asia Pet)

Guinea Pig Pneumonia (Bordetella / Strep.note: Enrofloxacin/TMS by culture, oxygen, fluids, nutrition (Asia Pet)

Dysecdysis (Incomplete Shed).correct: Increase humidity + warm soaks + gentle removal
Dysecdysis (Incomplete Shed).note: Warm soaks/humid hide, gently remove retained shed (esp. eye caps/tail tip), correct humidity & nutrition

Corneal Ulcer (Ulcerative Keratitis).note: Topical ofloxacin/chloramphenicol q2–6h; autologous serum for melting ulcer; E-collar mandatory; NO steroids

Conjunctivitis.note: Topical chloramphenicol/fusidic acid; anti-inflammatory if non-infectious; address underlying (allergy/virus)

Glaucoma.note: Topical timolol/dorzolamide/latanoprost; systemic mannitol if acute; enucleation for painful blind eye

Anterior Uveitis.note: Topical prednisolone acetate (NOT if ulcer) + atropine for cycloplegia; treat underlying cause (systemic/infectious)

Keratoconjunctivitis Sicca (Dry Eye / KC.note: Cyclosporine A (or tacrolimus) ophthalmic BID lifelong; artificial tears; treat secondary infection

Cataract.note: Phacoemulsification under GA if good candidate; pre-op ERG; manage uveitis post-op; monitor non-surgical patients

Nasolacrimal Duct Obstruction / Epiphora.correct: Nasolacrimal duct flush under sedation ± topical abx
Nasolacrimal Duct Obstruction / Epiphora.note: Cannulate and flush duct under sedation; topical antibiotic; investigate dental root cause in rabbits

Retrobulbar Abscess / Exophthalmos.note: Drain abscess (via oral/retrobulbar approach), long-course abx (enrofloxacin/TMS); investigate dental origin in rabbits

Corneal Lipidosis (Lipid Keratopathy).correct: Monitor ± diet change; keratectomy if vision-limiting
Corneal Lipidosis (Lipid Keratopathy).note: Often incidental; reduce dietary lipids (birds); superficial keratectomy if progressive; rule out systemic hyperlipidemia

Hyphema.correct: Treat underlying cause + topical anti-inflammatory + restrict activity
Hyphema.note: Address coagulopathy/trauma/hypertension; topical prednisolone + atropine if no ulcer; cage rest; monitor IOP (secondary glaucoma)

Osteoarthritis (Rabbit / Geriatric).note: Meloxicam long-term; joint supplements (glucosamine); soft bedding, ramps, weight control; acupuncture/physio adjunct

Perosis (Slipped Tendon).correct: Correct nutrition (Mn/biotin) + splint if early stage
Perosis (Slipped Tendon).note: Dietary correction (manganese, biotin, zinc, choline); hobble/splint early cases; established cases often irreversible

Intestinal Obstruction / Acute Gastric D.note: Stabilise (fluids, analgesia, decompression), emergency surgery for mechanical obstruction; decompress tympany via OG tube/trocar

Mucoid Enteropathy (Rabbit).note: Aggressive fluids, analgesia, prokinetics, warmth; cholestyramine sometimes used; address stress/diet; guarded prognosis in young rabbits

Myxomatosis.correct: Supportive care only (no specific antiviral; often fatal)
Myxomatosis.note: Intensive nursing: fluids, nutritional support, topical eye care, antibiotics for secondary infection; high mortality; prevention = vaccination + vector control

Tyzzer's Disease.correct: Supportive care (often fatal; metronidazole sometimes tried)
Tyzzer's Disease.note: Aggressive fluids + supportive; metronidazole or oxytetracycline sometimes tried; high mortality especially in young; improve hygiene/reduce stress

Rabbit Syphilis (Treponematosis).correct: Penicillin G benzathine IM (injectable — safe in rabbits)
Rabbit Syphilis (Treponematosis).note: Penicillin G benzathine 42,000–84,000 IU/kg SC/IM q7d × 3 doses; NEVER oral penicillin in rabbits; treat in-contact rabbits

EEHV (Elephant Endotheliotropic Herpesvi.note: IV famciclovir/acyclovir, IV fluids, plasma transfusion; extremely high mortality in young elephants; prevention = monitoring + early detection

Pregnancy Toxaemia (Rabbit).note: IV/IO dextrose, calcium gluconate, warmed fluids, assisted feeding; C-section if viable; prevent via late-gestation nutrition/avoid obesity

Iron Storage Disease (Hemochromatosis).note: Serial phlebotomy (1% BW) to deplete iron stores; low-iron diet (no citrus = no vitamin C enhancing absorption); deferoxamine if severe

White Muscle Disease (Nutritional Myopat.note: Injectable selenium + vitamin E; correct dietary deficiency; cardiac form may be fatal; skeletal form responds if treated early

Capture / Restraint Myopathy.note: IV fluids (alkalinise urine), diazepam if seizures, dark/quiet environment, minimize further handling; often fatal despite treatment

Arginine Deficiency Encephalopathy.note: IV arginine to reduce hyperammonemia; correct diet (add arginine source); cats/neonatal birds = obligate arginine requirement

Grass Tetany (Hypomagnesemia).note: IV/SC magnesium sulfate (slowly, cardiac monitoring); calcium gluconate if concurrent; prevent with Mg-supplemented pasture/diet

Thymoma.note: Surgical removal (median sternotomy) if resectable; prednisolone for associated signs (exfoliative dermatitis/hypercalcemia); radiation if available

Otitis Media / Interna (Rabbit).note: Enrofloxacin/TMS minimum 4–6 weeks (ideally culture-guided); NSAID; bulla osteotomy for refractory/abscess cases

Cardiomyopathy / Heart Failure (Rabbit).note: Furosemide for CHF, pimobendan for systolic dysfunction, ACE inhibitor adjunct; oxygen in acute; guarded long-term

Aortic / Soft-tissue Mineralization (Rab.note: Reduce dietary calcium and vitamin D; often incidental in older rabbits; treat secondary complications if symptomatic

Chronic Kidney Disease (Rabbit).note: Renal-support diet (low protein/Ca/P), SC/IV fluids, phosphate binder (aluminum hydroxide), manage anemia if severe

Cystitis / Hypercalciuria / Urine Scald.correct: Increase water intake + reduce Ca diet + flush bladder if sludge
Cystitis / Hypercalciuria / Urine Scald.note: Increase water (bottle + bowl), reduce dietary calcium (alfalfa→timothy), express/flush bladder sludge under sedation; analgesia; treat urine scald topically

Mastitis (Rabbit).correct: Systemic antibiotic + warm compress + express milk
Mastitis (Rabbit).note: Culture-guided abx (enrofloxacin/TMS); warm compresses, express affected gland; analgesia; may need surgical drainage; hand-rear kits if needed

Cheyletiellosis (Walking Dandruff).note: Selamectin (Revolution) or ivermectin repeated q2w × 3; treat in-contact animals and environment; investigate underlying grooming impairment

Sebaceous Adenitis (Rabbit).note: Cyclosporine (topical or systemic), emollient baths, keratolytic shampoo; biopsy to confirm; lifelong management

Cuterebra / Taenia serialis Cyst.correct: Surgical removal of larva/cyst + wound care
Cuterebra / Taenia serialis Cyst.note: Enlarge breathing pore (Cuterebra), extract intact, flush wound; excise coenurus cyst; systemic abx if secondary infection; ivermectin to prevent

Dysautonomia (Rabbit).note: Intensive supportive: fluids, assisted feeding, pilocarpine eye drops for mydriasis, prokinetics; prognosis guarded to poor

Aberrant Conjunctival Overgrowth.correct: Surgical resection if vision-impairing; otherwise monitor
Aberrant Conjunctival Overgrowth.note: Benign conjunctival tissue over cornea (lop/dwarf rabbits); surgical resection/debulking if obstructing vision; may recur

Hypervitaminosis D / Soft-tissue Mineral.correct: Remove vitamin D source + fluids + manage hypercalcemia
Hypervitaminosis D / Soft-tissue Mineral.note: Stop excess vitamin D supplementation; IV fluids, furosemide for hypercalcemia; calcitonin in severe cases; damage may be irreversible

Nutritional Encephalomalacia.note: Injectable vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol); correct diet to include adequate vitamin E; prognosis guarded if advanced cerebellar necrosis

Helicobacter Gastritis (Ferret).note: Triple therapy: amoxicillin 30 mg/kg PO BID + metronidazole 20 mg/kg PO BID + bismuth subsalicylate × 14–21 d; sucralfate for ulcer protection (Carpenter)

Mycobacteriosis (Avian/Reptile).correct: Euthanasia recommended (zoonotic, no cure); supportive if declined
Mycobacteriosis (Avian/Reptile).note: No effective cure in birds/reptiles; Mycobacterium avium complex — ZOONOTIC; euthanasia usually recommended; multi-drug antimycobacterial if owner declines

Salmonellosis (Reptile).correct: Enrofloxacin if septicemic + supportive; most reptiles are asymptomatic carriers
Salmonellosis (Reptile).note: Most healthy reptiles are asymptomatic carriers (ZOONOTIC); treat only clinically ill animals with culture-guided antibiotics; strict hygiene for owner

Haemoparasitosis (Avian/Reptile).note: Plasmodium: chloroquine/primaquine; Haemoproteus/Leucocytozoon: supportive + vector control; haemogregarines (reptile): usually nonpathogenic

Corneal Malacia (Melting Ulcer).correct: Emergency: topical abx q1–2h + autologous serum + NO steroids
Corneal Malacia (Melting Ulcer).note: Intensive topical broad-spectrum antibiotic (q1–2h), autologous serum or EDTA to inhibit collagenase, E-collar; NEVER steroids; conjunctival graft/surgery if perforating

Keratomycosis (Fungal Keratitis).note: Topical natamycin 5% or voriconazole 1%; superficial debridement; prolonged therapy; monitor for melting/perforation

Nutritional Secondary Hyperparathyroidis.correct: Calcium + vitamin D + UVB correction (= MBD treatment)
Nutritional Secondary Hyperparathyroidis.note: Correct dietary Ca:P ratio, provide UVB lighting; calcium support for acute cases; same principle as MBD

Zinc Deficiency.note: Zinc sulfate supplementation; correct diet to include adequate zinc (nuts, seeds for birds; varied diet for reptiles)

Copper Deficiency.note: Copper sulfate supplementation; correct diet; monitor hepatic copper in sensitive species

Sodium / Potassium Deficiency.correct: IV fluids with appropriate electrolyte replacement
Sodium / Potassium Deficiency.note: Correct electrolyte deficit with IV/IO fluids; identify and treat underlying cause (renal, GI, dietary); monitor closely

Selenium Toxicity.correct: Remove source + supportive care (no specific antidote)
Selenium Toxicity.note: Remove selenium source immediately; supportive care (fluids, hepatoprotection); no specific antidote; prognosis guarded if chronic

Hypovitaminosis B-complex.note: Injectable B-complex vitamins for acute cases; correct diet to include B-vitamin sources; thiamine deficiency in fish-fed birds/reptiles

Essential Fatty Acid Deficiency.note: Supplement omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids; correct all-seed or protein-deficient diets; improvement over weeks

Trichoblastoma (Rabbit).note: Complete surgical excision usually curative; benign hair follicle tumor common in older rabbits; histopathology to confirm

Squamous Cell Carcinoma (Exotic).correct: Wide surgical excision ± radiation if available
Squamous Cell Carcinoma (Exotic).note: Wide local excision with margins; radiation therapy if available; staging for metastasis; sun-exposed/non-pigmented areas predisposed

Hamster Lymphoma.note: Prednisolone for palliation; multi-agent chemo limited by size/compliance; staging first; guarded to poor prognosis

Anticoagulant Rodenticide Toxicosis.correct: Vitamin K1 (3–6 wk) + plasma/blood if bleeding
Anticoagulant Rodenticide Toxicosis.note: Phytonadione 2.5–5 mg/kg PO q12h (with fatty food) for 3–6 wk for 2nd-gen baits; fresh frozen plasma/whole blood + O2 if actively bleeding; activated charcoal if <4h; check PT 48–72h after stopping

Bromethalin Rodenticide Toxicosis.note: No antidote; induce emesis early then multi-dose activated charcoal; mannitol/diuretics + manage cerebral edema; anticonvulsants; cats highly sensitive; vitamin K does NOT work

Cholecalciferol Rodenticide Toxicosis.note: IV 0.9% saline diuresis + furosemide + glucocorticoids; pamidronate or calcitonin for hypercalcemia; low-Ca diet; monitor Ca×P for 2–4 wk; phosphate binders

Organophosphate / Carbamate Toxicosis.note: Atropine 0.1–0.5 mg/kg (¼ IV, rest IM/SC) to effect for SLUDGE/bradycardia; pralidoxime (2-PAM) 20 mg/kg for OP nicotinic signs (NOT for carbamates); bathe skin; diazepam for seizures

Pyrethroid Toxicosis (Permethrin — Cat).note: Methocarbamol 55–220 mg/kg slow IV for tremors; bathe with warm soapy water (dermal); diazepam/barbiturate for seizures; IV fluids + thermoregulation; intralipid for severe; NO permethrin on cats

Paracetamol (Acetaminophen) Toxicosis.note: NAC 140 mg/kg load then 70 mg/kg q6h ×7; O2; ± SAMe, vitamin C, ascorbate; methylene blue with caution (cats); cats exquisitely sensitive — even 1 tablet can be fatal

Ethylene Glycol Toxicosis.note: Fomepizole (dog 20 mg/kg load; cat needs high-dose 125 mg/kg) or ethanol CRI — works ONLY if very early (dog <8–12h, cat <3h); aggressive IV fluids; once anuric AKI established → hemodialysis/supportive

Grape / Raisin Toxicosis.note: Induce emesis + activated charcoal if recent; IV fluid diuresis 48h; monitor BUN/creat/Ca/P + urine output; no specific antidote; treat any AKI

Snake Envenomation.note: Polyvalent/species-specific antivenin as early as possible; keep animal calm + immobile; IV fluids, analgesia, ventilation for neurotoxic paralysis; monitor coagulation + CK; NO tourniquet/incision/suction

Toad Toxin Toxicosis.note: Flush mouth with copious water several minutes (avoid swallowing); atropine for hypersalivation/bradycardia; manage arrhythmias (cane/bufo = cardiac glycosides) + seizures; severe cases need ECG monitoring

Zinc Phosphide Rodenticide Toxicosis.note: No antidote; gastric lavage + activated charcoal in WELL-VENTILATED area (phosphine gas is a HUMAN hazard); antacids slow gas release; IV fluids + hepatoprotectants; control seizures; vomit is toxic to people
